definition: The New Keynesian Phillips Curve exhibits convexity near potential output, creating a fiscal-monetary policy trap. Last-mile disinflation from core PCE ~2.5-3% to target requires a sacrifice ratio of 4-5x, but the fiscal floor keeps the economy near potential and prevents the output gap from opening sufficiently. This is the Sargent-Wallace fiscal dominance problem in practice.
evidence: Core PCE stalling at ~2.5-3% after rapid decline from 5.6%
5Y5Y inflation breakevens at 2.3% and Michigan 5-10Y at 2.5% suggest partial de-anchoring
Fiscal floor prevents sufficient output gap opening
open_questions: Could a favorable supply shock (faster AI diffusion, immigration surge, commodity price decline) provide an exit without demand destruction?
How precise and regime-dependent is the 4-5x sacrifice ratio estimate?
